judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 correctly identifies and mitigates the RESULTS_DB collision risk (d) but fails to place F-11 in an early concurrent stage (b); Report 2 does the opposite, nailing the staged ordering but treating RESULTS_DB as inert and missing the collision risk — overall comparable, each missing one of four expectations.

report_1.evidence: Batch B — owns registry.py (one engineer): F-02 (add aurora-1 endpoint), F-05 (DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION → v3), F-09 (DEFAULT_DATASET → fixtures/eval_set.jsonl). Three independent config one-liners in one file.

report_2.evidence: Lane B — registry.py owner: F-02 (add aurora-1 endpoint), F-05 (DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION v1→v3), F-09 (DEFAULT_DATASET → eval_set.jsonl). Three edits to an 11-line file; giving all three to one person makes them trivial and conflict-free.
Wave 1 — the four lanes run fully in parallel (A, B, C, D). They touch disjoint files, so there's no cross-lane barrier.

report_1.evidence: F-06 — do not assign as written; escalate for a decision. "Tighten [PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS] so borderline answers score lower" is a subjective, output-facing behavior change with no target values given — it moves the score of every borderline answer. Picking the numbers is a product/quality-owner call, not something an engineer should decide unilaterally.

report_2.evidence: F-06 is a judgment/policy call, not a mechanical fix. "Partial-credit thresholds… are too lenient; tighten them" gives no target numbers... "Too lenient" is a subjective scoring-policy decision that changes every graded result, so it needs the rubric/product owner to specify the new values and sign off

report_1.evidence: Shared test database. tests/conftest.py defaults RESULTS_DB to /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db. Parallel test runs from different worktrees all point at that one path → they collide. Resolution: each engineer overrides RESULTS_DB to a private path.

report_2.evidence: Awareness of the RESULTS_DB env var. tests/conftest.py:4 reads RESULTS_DB (default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db). Nothing currently consumes it, but engineers adding tests should know it exists in case a new test wires it in.
